name: Barbarian
description: A fierce warrior who channels primal rage into extraordinary might and resilience, shrugging off blows that would fell lesser fighters.

features.name: Rage
features.description:
You can imbue yourself with a primal power called Rage, a force that grants you extraordinary might and resilience. You can enter it as a {rule:Bonus Action} if you aren't wearing Heavy armor.

You can enter your Rage the number of times shown for your Barbarian level in the Rages column of the Barbarian Features table. You regain one expended use when you finish a {rule:Short Rest}, and you regain all expended uses when you finish a {rule:Long Rest}.

While active, your Rage follows the rules below.

**Damage Resistance.** You have {rule:Resistance|resistance} to Bludgeoning, Piercing, and Slashing damage.

**Rage Damage.** When you make an attack using Strength—with either a weapon or an Unarmed Strike—and deal damage to the target, you gain a bonus to the damage that increases as you gain levels as a Barbarian, as shown in the Rage Damage column of the Barbarian Features table.

**Strength Advantage.** You have {rule:Advantage} on Strength checks and Strength {rule:Saving Throw|saving throws}.

**No Concentration or Spells.** You can't maintain {status:Concentration}, and you can't cast spells.

**Duration.** The Rage lasts until the end of your next turn, and it ends early if you don Heavy armor or have the {condition:Incapacitated} condition. If your Rage is still active on your next turn, you can extend the Rage for another round by doing one of the following: make an attack roll against an enemy; force an enemy to make a saving throw; or take a {rule:Bonus Action} to extend your Rage. Each time the Rage is extended, it lasts until the end of your next turn. You can maintain a Rage for up to 10 minutes.

properties.text: Can't maintain Concentration or cast spells while raging.
Rage ends early if you don Heavy armor or gain the Incapacitated condition.

features.name: Unarmored Defense
features.description: While you aren't wearing any armor, your base {rule:Armor Class} equals 10 plus your Dexterity and Constitution modifiers. You can use a Shield and still gain this benefit.

features.name: Weapon Mastery
features.description:
Your training with weapons allows you to use the mastery properties of two kinds of Simple or Martial Melee weapons of your choice, such as Greataxes and Handaxes. Whenever you finish a {rule:Long Rest}, you can practice weapon drills and change one of those weapon choices.

When you reach certain Barbarian levels, you gain the ability to use the mastery properties of more kinds of weapons, as shown in the Weapon Mastery column of the Barbarian Features table.

properties.text: You can use the mastery properties of a number of Simple or Martial Melee weapons equal to table(1:2, 4:3, 10:4) for your Barbarian level. You can change one weapon choice on each Long Rest.

features.name: Danger Sense
features.description: You gain an uncanny sense of when things aren't as they should be, giving you an edge when you dodge perils. You have {rule:Advantage} on Dexterity {rule:Saving Throw|saving throws} unless you have the {condition:Incapacitated} condition.

features.name: Reckless Attack
features.description: You can throw aside all concern for defense to attack with increased ferocity. When you make your first attack roll on your turn, you can decide to attack recklessly. Doing so gives you {rule:Advantage} on attack rolls using Strength until the start of your next turn, but attack rolls against you have {rule:Advantage} during that time.

properties.text: When you make your first attack on your turn, you can attack recklessly: gain advantage on Strength-based attack rolls until the start of your next turn, but attack rolls against you also have advantage.

features.name: Barbarian Subclass
features.description: You gain a Barbarian subclass of your choice. A subclass is a specialization that grants you features at certain Barbarian levels. For the rest of your career, you gain each of your subclass's features that are of your Barbarian level or lower.

features.name: Primal Knowledge
features.description:
You gain proficiency in another skill of your choice from the skill list available to Barbarians at level 1.

In addition, while your Rage is active, you can channel primal power when you attempt certain tasks; whenever you make an ability check using one of the following skills, you can make it as a Strength check even if it normally uses a different ability: Acrobatics, Intimidation, Perception, Stealth, or Survival. When you use this ability, your Strength represents primal power coursing through you, honing your agility, bearing, and senses.

properties.text: While raging, you can make Acrobatics, Intimidation, Perception, Stealth, or Survival checks as Strength checks.

features.name: Ability Score Improvement
features.description: You gain the {feat:Ability Score Improvement} feat or another feat of your choice for which you qualify. You gain this feature again at Barbarian levels 8, 12, and 16.

features.name: Extra Attack
features.description: You can attack twice instead of once whenever you take the {action:Attack} action on your turn.

features.name: Fast Movement
features.description: Your speed increases by 10 feet while you aren't wearing Heavy armor.

features.name: Subclass Feature
features.description: You gain a feature from your Barbarian subclass.

features.name: Feral Instinct
features.description: Your instincts are so honed that you have {rule:Advantage} on {rule:Initiative} rolls.

features.name: Instinctive Pounce
features.description: As part of the {rule:Bonus Action} you take to enter your Rage, you can move up to half your {rule:Speed}.

features.name: Ability Score Improvement
features.description: You gain the {feat:Ability Score Improvement} feat or another feat of your choice for which you qualify.

features.name: Brutal Strike
features.description:
If you use Reckless Attack, you can forgo any {rule:Advantage} on one Strength-based attack roll of your choice on your turn. The chosen attack roll mustn't have {rule:Disadvantage}. If the chosen attack roll hits, the target takes an extra 1d10 damage of the same type dealt by the weapon or Unarmed Strike, and you can cause one Brutal Strike effect of your choice. You have the following effect options.

**Forceful Blow.** The target is pushed 15 feet straight away from you. You can then move up to half your {rule:Speed} straight toward the target without provoking {action:Opportunity Attack|Opportunity Attacks}.

**Hamstring Blow.** The target's {rule:Speed} is reduced by 15 feet until the start of your next turn. A target can be affected by only one Hamstring Blow at a time—the most recent one.

properties.text: When using Reckless Attack, you can forgo advantage on one Strength-based attack roll. If it hits, target takes extra 1d10 damage and suffers one Brutal Strike effect: Forceful Blow (pushed 15 ft) or Hamstring Blow (speed reduced 15 ft until start of your next turn).

features.name: Subclass Feature
features.description: You gain a feature from your Barbarian subclass.

features.name: Relentless Rage
features.description:
Your Rage can keep you fighting despite grievous wounds. If you drop to 0 {rule:Hit Points} while your Rage is active and don't die outright, you can make a DC 10 Constitution {rule:Saving Throw}. If you succeed, your Hit Points instead change to a number equal to twice your Barbarian level.

Each time you use this feature after the first, the DC increases by 5. When you finish a {rule:Short Rest} or {rule:Long Rest}, the DC resets to 10.

properties.text: When you drop to 0 HP while raging, make a DC 10 CON save (DC +5 each subsequent use, resets on Short/Long Rest). On success, HP instead becomes 2 × Barbarian level.

features.name: Ability Score Improvement
features.description: You gain the {feat:Ability Score Improvement} feat or another feat of your choice for which you qualify.

features.name: Improved Brutal Strike
features.description:
You have honed new ways to attack furiously. The following effects are now among your Brutal Strike options.

**Staggering Blow.** The target has {rule:Disadvantage} on the next saving throw it makes, and it can't make {action:Opportunity Attack|Opportunity Attacks} until the start of your next turn.

**Sundering Blow.** Before the start of your next turn, the next attack roll made by another creature against the target gains a +5 bonus to the roll. An attack roll can gain only one Sundering Blow bonus.

properties.text: Two additional Brutal Strike options: Staggering Blow (target has disadvantage on next save and can't make opportunity attacks until start of your next turn) and Sundering Blow (next attack roll by another creature against the target gains +5).

features.name: Subclass Feature
features.description: You gain a feature from your Barbarian subclass.

features.name: Persistent Rage
features.description:
When you roll {rule:Initiative}, you can regain all expended uses of Rage. After you regain uses of Rage in this way, you can't do so again until you finish a {rule:Long Rest}.

In addition, your Rage is so fierce that it now lasts for 10 minutes without you needing to do anything to extend it from round to round. Your Rage ends early if you have the {condition:Unconscious} condition (not just the {condition:Incapacitated} condition) or don Heavy armor.

properties.text: On Initiative roll, can regain all expended Rage uses (once per Long Rest). Rage no longer requires extension actions — it lasts 10 minutes automatically, ending only on Unconscious condition or donning Heavy armor.

features.name: Ability Score Improvement
features.description: You gain the {feat:Ability Score Improvement} feat or another feat of your choice for which you qualify.

features.name: Improved Brutal Strike
features.description: The extra damage of your Brutal Strike increases to 2d10. In addition, you can use two different Brutal Strike effects whenever you use your Brutal Strike feature.

properties.text: Brutal Strike extra damage increases to 2d10. You can now apply two different Brutal Strike effects on the same hit.

features.name: Indomitable Might
features.description: If your total for a Strength check or Strength {rule:Saving Throw} is less than your Strength score, you can use that score in place of the total.

properties.text: For Strength checks and Strength saving throws, you can use your Strength score instead of the roll total if the score is higher.

features.name: Epic Boon
features.description: You gain an Epic Boon feat or another feat of your choice for which you qualify. Boon of Irresistible Offense is recommended.

features.name: Primal Champion
features.description: You embody primal power. Your Strength and Constitution scores increase by 4, to a maximum of 25.

properties.text: Strength and Constitution scores each increase by 4, to a maximum of 25. Recalculate HP with the new Constitution modifier.

subclasses.name: Path of the Berserker
subclasses.description: Barbarians who walk the Path of the Berserker direct their Rage primarily toward violence. Their path is one of untrammeled fury, and they thrill in the chaos of battle as they allow their Rage to seize and empower them.

features.name: Frenzy
features.description: If you use Reckless Attack while your Rage is active, you deal extra damage to the first target you hit on your turn with a Strength-based attack. To determine the extra damage, roll a number of d6s equal to your Rage Damage bonus, and add them together. The damage has the same type as the weapon or Unarmed Strike used for the attack.

properties.text: When you use Reckless Attack while raging, the first Strength-based attack hit on your turn deals extra damage equal to Rage Damage bonus in d6s (e.g., at level 1-8: 2d6, level 9-15: 3d6, level 16+: 4d6).

features.name: Mindless Rage
features.description: You have {rule:Immunity|immunity} to the {condition:Charmed} and {condition:Frightened} conditions while your Rage is active. If you're {condition:Charmed} or {condition:Frightened} when you enter your Rage, the condition ends on you.

properties.text: If Charmed or Frightened when you enter Rage, those conditions end on you.

features.name: Retaliation
features.description: When you take damage from a creature that is within 5 feet of you, you can take a {rule:Reaction} to make one melee attack against that creature, using a weapon or an Unarmed Strike.

properties.text: Reaction: when you take damage from a creature within 5 feet, make one melee attack (weapon or Unarmed Strike) against it.

features.name: Intimidating Presence
features.description:
As a {rule:Bonus Action}, you can strike terror into others with your menacing presence and primal power. When you do so, each creature of your choice in a 30-foot Emanation originating from you must make a Wisdom {rule:Saving Throw} (DC 8 plus your Strength modifier and Proficiency Bonus). On a failed save, a creature has the {condition:Frightened} condition for 1 minute. At the end of each of the {condition:Frightened} creature's turns, the creature repeats the save, ending the effect on itself on a success.

Once you use this feature, you can't use it again until you finish a {rule:Long Rest} unless you expend a use of your Rage (no action required) to restore your use of it.

properties.text: Bonus Action: creatures of your choice in 30-ft Emanation make WIS save (DC 8 + STR mod + Prof). Fail: Frightened 1 minute (repeating save each turn). Can expend a Rage use to restore this feature without resting.

subclasses.name: Path of the Wild Heart
subclasses.description: Barbarians who follow the Path of the Wild Heart view themselves as kin to animals. These Barbarians learn magical means to communicate with animals, and their Rage heightens their connection to animals as it fills them with supernatural might.

features.name: Animal Speaker
features.description: You can cast the {spell:Beast Sense} and {spell:Speak with Animals} spells but only as Rituals. Wisdom is your spellcasting ability for them.

properties.text: Beast Sense and Speak with Animals can only be cast as Rituals (not using spell slots).

features.name: Rage of the Wilds
features.description:
Your Rage taps into the primal power of animals. Whenever you activate your Rage, you gain one of the following options of your choice.

**Bear.** While your Rage is active, you have {rule:Resistance|resistance} to every damage type except Force, Necrotic, Psychic, and Radiant.

**Eagle.** When you activate your Rage, you can take the {action:Disengage} and {action:Dash} actions as part of that {rule:Bonus Action}. While your Rage is active, you can take a {rule:Bonus Action} to take both of those actions.

**Wolf.** While your Rage is active, your allies have {rule:Advantage} on attack rolls against any enemy of yours within 5 feet of you.

properties.text: On Rage activation, take Disengage and Dash as part of that Bonus Action. While raging, can take Bonus Action to take both Disengage and Dash.
While raging, allies have advantage on attack rolls against any enemy within 5 feet of you.

features.name: Aspect of the Wilds
features.description:
You gain one of the following options of your choice. Whenever you finish a {rule:Long Rest}, you can change your choice.

**Owl.** You have Darkvision with a range of 60 feet. If you already have Darkvision, its range increases by 60 feet.

**Panther.** You have a Climb Speed equal to your {rule:Speed}.

**Salmon.** You have a Swim Speed equal to your {rule:Speed}.

properties.text: If you already have Darkvision, its range increases by 60 feet instead.

features.name: Nature Speaker
features.description: You can cast the {spell:Commune with Nature} spell but only as a {rule:Ritual}. Wisdom is your spellcasting ability for it.

properties.text: Commune with Nature can only be cast as a Ritual (not using a spell slot).

features.name: Power of the Wilds
features.description:
Whenever you activate your Rage, you gain one of the following options of your choice.

**Falcon.** While your Rage is active, you have a Fly Speed equal to your {rule:Speed} if you aren't wearing any armor.

**Lion.** While your Rage is active, any of your enemies within 5 feet of you have {rule:Disadvantage} on attack rolls against targets other than you or another Barbarian who has this option active.

**Ram.** While your Rage is active, you can cause a Large or smaller creature to have the {condition:Prone} condition when you hit it with a melee attack.

properties.text: While raging, enemies within 5 feet of you have disadvantage on attack rolls against targets other than you or another Barbarian who has this option active.
While raging, when you hit a Large or smaller creature with a melee attack, you can give it the Prone condition.

subclasses.name: Path of the World Tree
subclasses.description: Barbarians who follow the Path of the World Tree connect with the cosmic tree Yggdrasil through their Rage. This tree grows among the Outer Planes, connecting them to each other and the Material Plane. These Barbarians draw on the tree's magic for vitality and as a means of dimensional travel.

features.name: Vitality of the Tree
features.description:
Your Rage taps into the life force of the World Tree. You gain the following benefits.

**Vitality Surge.** When you activate your Rage, you gain a number of {rule:Temporary Hit Points} equal to your Barbarian level.

**Life-Giving Force.** At the start of each of your turns while your Rage is active, you can choose another creature within 10 feet of yourself to gain Temporary Hit Points. To determine the number of Temporary Hit Points, roll a number of d6s equal to your Rage Damage bonus, and add them together. If any of these Temporary Hit Points remain when your Rage ends, they vanish.

properties.text: On Rage activation, gain Temporary HP equal to Barbarian level. While raging, at start of each of your turns you can grant another creature within 10 ft Temporary HP equal to Rage Damage bonus in d6s. These bonus Temporary HPs vanish when Rage ends.

features.name: Branches of the Tree
features.description: Whenever a creature you can see starts its turn within 30 feet of you while your Rage is active, you can take a {rule:Reaction} to summon spectral branches of the World Tree around it. The target must succeed on a Strength {rule:Saving Throw} (DC 8 plus your Strength modifier and Proficiency Bonus) or be teleported to an unoccupied space you can see within 5 feet of yourself or in the nearest unoccupied space you can see. After the target teleports, you can reduce its {rule:Speed} to 0 until the end of the current turn.

properties.text: Reaction: while raging, when a creature you can see starts its turn within 30 ft, force a STR save (DC 8 + STR mod + Prof). Fail: teleported to within 5 ft of you. After teleport, you can reduce its Speed to 0 until end of that turn.

features.name: Battering Roots
features.description: During your turn, your reach is 10 feet greater with any Melee weapon that has the Heavy or Versatile property, as tendrils of the World Tree extend from you. When you hit with such a weapon on your turn, you can activate the Push or Topple mastery property in addition to a different mastery property you're using with that weapon.

properties.text: +10 ft reach on your turn with Heavy or Versatile melee weapons. On a hit with such a weapon, you can activate Push or Topple mastery property in addition to any other mastery property you're using.

features.name: Travel Along the Tree
features.description:
When you activate your Rage and as a {rule:Bonus Action} while your Rage is active, you can teleport up to 60 feet to an unoccupied space you can see.

In addition, once per Rage, you can increase the range of that teleport to 150 feet. When you do so, you can also bring up to six willing creatures who are within 10 feet of you. Each creature teleports to an unoccupied space of your choice within 10 feet of your destination space.

properties.text: On Rage activation and as a Bonus Action while raging, teleport up to 60 ft to an unoccupied space you can see. Once per Rage, extend that teleport to 150 ft and bring up to 6 willing creatures within 10 ft; each appears within 10 ft of your destination.

subclasses.name: Path of the Zealot
subclasses.description: Barbarians who walk the Path of the Zealot receive boons from a god or pantheon. These Barbarians experience their Rage as an ecstatic episode of divine union that infuses them with power. They are often allies to the priests and other followers of their god or pantheon.

features.name: Divine Fury
features.description: You can channel divine power into your strikes. On each of your turns while your Rage is active, the first creature you hit with a weapon or an Unarmed Strike takes extra damage equal to 1d6 plus half your Barbarian level (round down). The extra damage is Necrotic or Radiant; you choose the type each time you deal the damage.

properties.text: While raging, the first creature you hit each turn with a weapon or Unarmed Strike takes extra damage equal to 1d6 + half Barbarian level (rounded down). Choose Necrotic or Radiant each time.

features.name: Warrior of the Gods
features.description:
A divine entity helps ensure you can continue the fight. You have a pool of four d12s that you can spend to heal yourself. As a {rule:Bonus Action}, you can expend dice from the pool, roll them, and regain a number of {rule:Hit Points} equal to the roll's total.

Your pool regains all expended dice when you finish a {rule:Long Rest}. The pool's maximum number of dice increases by one when you reach Barbarian levels 6 (5 dice), 12 (6 dice), and 17 (7 dice).

properties.text: Pool of d12s (4 at level 3, 5 at 6, 6 at 12, 7 at 17). Bonus Action: expend any number of dice from pool, roll them, regain that many HP.

features.name: Fanatical Focus
features.description: Once per active Rage, if you fail a saving throw, you can reroll it with a bonus equal to your Rage Damage bonus, and you must use the new roll.

properties.text: Once per Rage: when you fail a saving throw, reroll it and add your Rage Damage bonus to the new roll. You must use the new result.

features.name: Zealous Presence
features.description:
As a {rule:Bonus Action}, you unleash a battle cry infused with divine energy. Up to ten other creatures of your choice within 60 feet of you gain {rule:Advantage} on attack rolls and {rule:Saving Throw|saving throws} until the start of your next turn.

Once you use this feature, you can't use it again until you finish a {rule:Long Rest} unless you expend a use of your Rage (no action required) to restore your use of it.

properties.text: Bonus Action: up to 10 other creatures of your choice within 60 ft gain advantage on attack rolls and saving throws until start of your next turn. Can expend a Rage use to restore this feature without resting.

features.name: Rage of the Gods
features.description:
When you activate your Rage, you can assume the form of a divine warrior. This form lasts for 1 minute or until you drop to 0 {rule:Hit Points}. Once you use this feature, you can't do so again until you finish a {rule:Long Rest}.

While in this form, you gain the benefits below.

**Flight.** You have a Fly Speed equal to your {rule:Speed} and can hover.

**Resistance.** You have {rule:Resistance|resistance} to Necrotic, Psychic, and Radiant damage.

**Revivification.** When a creature within 30 feet of you would drop to 0 {rule:Hit Points}, you can take a {rule:Reaction} to expend a use of your Rage to instead change the target's Hit Points to a number equal to your Barbarian level.

properties.text: Fly Speed equals Speed and you can hover.
Reaction: expend a Rage use to set a creature within 30 ft that would drop to 0 HP to Barbarian level HP instead.
